pi_items.source: export_result.sh checks for this specific path and task.json instructions explicitly request it
task.json instructions explicitly require setting gravity to 3.72, which matches Mars' approximate surface gravity
task.json instructions require setting basicTimeStep to 16
task.json instructions require setting camera width to 1024
task.json instructions require setting camera height to 768
task.json instructions require setting rover mass to 180.0 kg
task.json instructions require adding a ContactProperties node with coulombFriction [0.4]
setup_task.sh generates the initial world file with 'DEF MARS_ROVER Robot'
setup_task.sh generates the initial camera node with 'DEF nav_camera Camera'
task.json description explicitly defines this role
task.json context strongly aligns with this industry classification

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated Webots world file created by setup_task.sh. The simulated world is generated with placeholder parameters (e.g. Earth's gravity 9.81 m/s², timestep 64) and instructs the agent to manually reconfigure them for a Mars rover. I verified that all parameter modifications—gravity 3.72, timestep 16, camera resolution 1024x768, rover mass 180.0 kg, and adding wheel-regolith friction 0.4—exactly match the explicit instructions in task.json and the checking logic within verifier.py. The target scene graph identifiers 'MARS_ROVER' and 'nav_camera' were also confirmed via the synthetic world template in the setup script.
